name: schema-validation
context: Enforce MongoDB document shape with validators, `$jsonSchema`, and invalid-write policies
info: MongoDB does not require a fixed schema, but it does support server-side validation through collection validators. This node covers creating and modifying validators with `$jsonSchema`, selecting `validationAction` (`error` or `warn`), choosing `validationLevel` for how strictly existing documents are treated, and using BSON-aware field rules such as `bsonType`, `required`, and nested property constraints. Agents use it when flexibility is becoming drift, when writes must be rejected consistently, or when a collection needs a gradual cleanup path.
triggers: add a validator to a collection

sections.title: JSON schema rule vocabulary
sections.body: MongoDB validators use a BSON-aware JSON schema dialect. `bsonType` lets you distinguish MongoDB-specific types, `required` enforces required fields, `properties` defines nested rules, and `enum`, `minimum`, `maximum`, `pattern`, and `additionalProperties` can refine constraints further. Use Extended JSON examples in documentation so agents can express ObjectIds and dates consistently.

sections.title: Choosing validation action
sections.body: Use `validationAction: "error"` when invalid writes must be blocked immediately. Use `validationAction: "warn"` when you need observability first and a later cleanup or backfill. Warning mode is especially useful when retrofitting validators onto a collection that already has inconsistent historical data.

sections.title: Choosing validation level
sections.body: Use `validationLevel: "strict"` for clean collections and new workloads. Use `validationLevel: "moderate"` when older invalid documents still exist and you want validation to focus on new inserts and updates that touch compliant documents. Moderate mode is a migration aid, not a permanent substitute for cleanup.

links.context: Write paths should align with collection validators
Validation and indexing often evolve together as a collection matures

related.note: Validators are only useful if the write paths surface and handle validation failures correctly
Zod complements MongoDB validators by enforcing shape in application code before the write reaches the database

author: dojo-community
